title: Technical English Practice Set
description: Backend-scored recall for behavioral answers, tradeoff language, and production-facing English.

questions.prompt: Which phrase sounds the most professional when describing a technical issue in review?
questions.explanation: The strongest option identifies the risk, explains it calmly, and avoids vague or emotional phrasing.

options.label: This shader is bad and should be redone.
I do not like this setup at all.
Maybe it is kind of inefficient or something.
The current setup reads well, but I think the cost is risky for repeated use.

questions.prompt: What is the most useful structure for a behavioral answer?
questions.explanation: A compact context -> constraint -> action -> result structure keeps answers clear and relevant.

options.label: Opinion -> opinion -> conclusion
Context -> constraint -> action -> result
Introduction -> joke -> technical deep dive
Random highlights from the project

questions.prompt: Which word is most appropriate when describing a lower-cost alternative version of a shader?
questions.explanation: Fallback is a common production term for a cheaper or safer alternative path.

options.label: Decoration
Emotion
Fallback
Improvisation

questions.prompt: Which sentence best communicates a tradeoff without sounding defensive?
questions.explanation: This phrasing respects the visual goal, states the risk, and offers a practical next move.

options.label: The effect reads well, but I think the current cost is risky for repeated use.
We cannot do this because it is too annoying to optimize.
The art team should lower their expectations.
I guess it is maybe too expensive or something.

questions.prompt: Which phrase sounds strongest in an interview when explaining your contribution?
questions.explanation: This version shows context, action, and impact without sounding like you are claiming the entire project alone.

options.label: I basically saved the whole project by myself.
I built a small tool that reduced iteration time for the artists.
I did a lot of things and everyone liked it.
I am good at many software packages.

questions.prompt: When speaking to an international team, which term is most useful for describing a slow content pipeline?
questions.explanation: Iteration time is a precise and common production phrase for how long it takes to test and revise work.

options.label: Decoration speed
Emotional pacing
Visual excitement
Iteration time

questions.prompt: Which sentence sounds most senior when proposing a compromise for low-end hardware?
questions.explanation: This answer keeps the visual goal, acknowledges hardware limits, and proposes a scalable solution.

options.label: We should just remove the whole effect.
The effect is cool but probably impossible.
We can keep the hero version and add a cheaper fallback for broader use.
The hardware is bad, so there is nothing to do.
